generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T CASSADA. Manihot utilissima, Pohl (Jatropha manihot, L.) [The bitter variety is poisonous, differing from the sweet, as bitter from sweet almonds (the difference probably due to the presence of hydrocianic acid); botanically, the two varieties are alike.] Nat. order , Euphorbiaceæ. Common name , Sweet or bitter cassada or cassava. Preparation , Tincture of the root. Authorities. 1 , 2 , 3 , Reil, Journ. f. Pharm., Tox., and Therap., 1, 586; review of Michiel de Keyzer, Inaug. dis., "Bydrage tot de Kennis van het Blauwzuur," 1856, poisoning of two boys and a girl, aged respectively 3, 4, and 6 years; 4 , ibid., poisoning of a negro by the juice; 5 , Dr. Barham, general statement of symptoms of poisoning, Pharm, Journ., 5, 29, 1846; 6 , Mure, Pathogénésie Brésilienne, effects from an ounce dose of the expressed juice of the root, by J. V. Martins. MIND. ► Emotional. ► At times, painful weeping, with grinding of the teeth, [3] . ► Sadness, [6] . ► Wakes in bad humor (second day), [6] . ► Intellectual. ► She seemed to have some consciousness, as was shown by the look of her eye when her name was called aloud, [3] . ► Senseless and unconscious, with ice-cold hands and feet, pale, sunken face, half-closed eyes, dilated pupils, half-opened mouth, full of saliva, respiration very slow, almost imperceptible, action of the heart intermitting, scarcely perceptible, anus relaxed, wide open, limbs relaxed, [1] , [2] . ► He fell to the ground unconscious, with loud cries and convulsions, [4] . HEAD. ► Dizziness (second day), [6] . ► Giddiness of the head, [5] . ► Weight in the head, especially the top of the forehead, at 4 P.M. (second day), [6] . ► Pain in the forehead and nasal fossæ (fourth day), [6] . EYE. ► Pain within the orbits, [6] . ► Dimness of sight, [5] . ► Optical illusions (fourth day), [6] . EAR. ► Noise in the ears, extending to the occiput, like letting off steam (third day), [6] . MOUTH. ► Clammy mouth, with bad breath (third day), [6] . ► Bitter mouth (fourth day), [6] . THROAT. ► Sense of swelling in the left tonsil (second day), [6] . ► Pain at the velum palati, [6] . ► Slight feeling of dryness in the sophagus, [6] . STOMACH. [20.] ► Appetite when beginning breakfast, but it suddenly ceases after taking a little milk (second day), [6] . ► Thirst, [6] . ► Eructations at 6.30 A.M., [6] . ► Violent vomiting and purging, [5] . ► Heaviness in the stomach all night, relieved by applying the hands (fourth day), [6] . ► Slight weight in the stomach, [6] . ► Pain and sickness of the stomach, [5] . ► Slight dull pains in stomach and bowels, with borborygmi (second day), [6] . ► Vague and transient, but very acute, pains in the stomach (third day), [6] . ABDOMEN. ► Swelling of the whole abdomen, [5] . [30.] ► Vague pains in the hypogastrium, when urinating, at 6 A.M. (second day), [6] . RECTUM AND ANUS. ► Tenesmus and pressure in the sphincter ani, with pricking, at 3 P.M., (second day), [6] . ► At 8 o'clock, renewed, but ineffectual urging to stool (second day), [6] . STOOL. ► Diarrha in the morning (sixth day), [6] . ► Unusually easy stools, [6] . ► Watery, greenish, fetid stools (second day), [6] . ► Copious, light-colored, watery, fetid stools (second day), [6] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Sudden pain, with lancination, in the urethra, above the fossa navicularis, for two minutes (second day), [6] . ► Slight heat in the urethra (third day), [6] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. ► Respiration intermitting, at times almost ceasing, [3] . CHEST. [40.] ► Pain within the chest, [6] . ► Pain in the side of the chest and in the shoulders, aggravated by movement (second day), [6] . HEART AND PULSE. ► Pulse very weak, [3] . BACK. ► Pain in the back (third day), [6] . ► Pain at the loins (sixth day), [6] . SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Acute pain in the left arm at night, [6] . ► Pain in the left elbow (third day), [6] . INFERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Pain at the inside of the thigh (fifth day), [6] . ► Rheumatic pain in the right thigh (eighth day), [6] . ► Trembling of the knees and of all the limbs with violent emotion, when hearing others talk about the disease he is afraid of having (second day), [6] . [50.] ► Weakness of the knees when going upstairs, [6] . ► Swelling of the ankles, rather less at night (third day), [6] . GENERAL SYMPTOMS. ► Slight cramps, with staring look, [3] . ► Debility, [6] . ► Moral and physical prostration (third day), [6] . ► Faintings, [5] . SLEEP AND DREAMS. ► Sleepiness. ► Drowsiness (sixth day), [6] . ► Drowsiness in daytime, [6] . ► Sleep at 4 P.M. (second day), [6] . ► Falls asleep late, [6] . [60.] ► Distressing dreams, about wanting to save an asphyxiated child, whose parents will not let him treat it (second day), [6] . ► Dreams about a conflagration, with small flames, like those presented by the 10th attenuation of Crotalus cascavella, when examined under the solar microscope, at the Homopathic Institute of Rio Janeiro (fifth day), [6] . FEVER. ► Coldness and shrinking, [5] . ► The whole head, especially the nape of the neck, is cold (eighth day), [6] . ► Face and limbs cold, [3] . ► Icy coldness under the shoulder-blades, in bed, even when well covered up (seventh day), [6] . ► Icy coldness of the arm, even to the marrow of the bone (eighthday), [6] . ► Icy coldness of the knees (sixth day), [6] . ► Cold feet and hands (seventh day), [6] . ► Profuse sweat during sleep, ceasing on waking (second day), [6] . [70.] ► Very fetid sweat under the axillæ and on the scrotum (third day), [6] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Morning ), 6.30 o'clock, eructations; 6 o'clock, when urinating, pain in hypogastrium; diarrha. ► ( Afternoon ), 4 o'clock, weight in head; 3 o'clock, tenesmus, etc. ► ( Night ), Pain in left arm. ► ( Movement ), Pain in chest. ► ( During sleep ), Sweat. ► Amelioration. ► ( Night ), Swelling of the ankles. ► ( Applying hands to part ), Heaviness in stomach.
